data.title: Jaguar Uranium Corp signs deal with Mendoza's Ministry of Energy and Environment; subsidiary forges collaboration agreement.
data.description: Toronto-based Jaguar Uranium Corp. revealed on March 4, 2026, that its subsidiary 2847312 Ontario Inc. has signed a non-exclusive framework collaboration deal with the Ministry of Energy. The company trades on the NYSE American under the symbol JAGU.

data.title: Morgan Stanley selects Coinbase and BNY as custodians for upcoming Bitcoin ETF, advancing its crypto adoption.
data.description: Morgan Stanley has disclosed the custodians for its Bitcoin ETF as the renowned Wall Street firm progresses towards a comprehensive adoption of cryptocurrencies. The revelation marks a significant step in the financial institution's crypto strategy, highlighting its commitment to entering the digital asset space. This latest development from Morgan Stanley showcases a clear intention to engage with the growing crypto market.

data.title: NSCAD workers in Halifax go on strike as negotiations fail. CUPE Local 3912 demands better job security and rights.
data.description: Employees of Nova Scotia College of Art and Design in Halifax, NS, who are members of Canadian Union of Public Employees (CUPE) Local 3912, have begun a strike after negotiations for their initial collective agreement broke down recently. Lachlan Sheldrick, the vice president of CUPE 3912, emphasized the importance of addressing the job insecurity faced by CUPE members. The striking workers include teaching assistants and others seeking standard protections comparable to those enjoyed by employees at other universities in the Halifax area.

data.title: CUPE members rally against Sienna Senior Living for cutting 1300 full-time hours at Case Manor in Bobcaygeon, despite $4.9 billion Ontario government investment in PSWs.
data.description: In Markham, Canada, Sienna Senior Living is a highly successful Long Term Care provider operating nationwide, receiving government support and public investments. Even with Ontario government allocating $4.9 billion for PSWs staffing, Sienna has decided to reduce 1300 full-time hours at their Case Manor facility in Bobcaygeon ON, causing concern.

data.title: WWP CEO Lt. Gen. (Ret.) Walt Piatt testified at Senate and House VA Committees, urges passing Major Richard Star Act.
data.description: Retired Lieutenant General Walt Piatt, CEO of Wounded Warrior Project (WWP), is calling on Congress to pass the Major Richard Star Act. Piatt stressed the importance of supporting current and future combat veterans in addition to honoring those from the past. This call to action was made on March 4, 2026 in Washington, D.C. The Major Richard Star Act aims to provide better assistance to combat veterans.

data.title: Olé Cocktail Co. extends partnership with ASICS Los Angeles Marathon, emphasizing active lifestyle support.
data.description: On March 4, 2026, the ASICS Los Angeles Marathon, managed by The McCourt Foundation, revealed that Olé Cocktail Co.(TM), the high-quality tequila cocktail and mocktail brand under Mark Anthony Brands, will be back as an official partner.

data.title: MediWound (MDWD) to report Q1 earnings on 03/05 with estimated EPS of -$0.63. Historical performance shows previous EPS and price changes. Stock trading at $17.2, up 9.22% in the last 52 weeks.
data.description: MediWound (NASDAQ: MDWD) is set to announce its latest quarterly earnings on March 5, 2026. Analysts predict an EPS of $-0.63. In the previous quarter, the company exceeded expectations by $0.58, resulting in a 1.19% increase in share price. MediWound's stock was trading at $17.2 on March 3, with a 52-week increase of 9.22%. Investors anticipate positive news exceeding estimates and optimistic guidance for the next quarter, as guidance can strongly influence stock prices. Understanding past performance is essential for new investors. Full article available at Benzinga.com.
